message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The lateral flow assay format enables rapid, instrument-free, at-home testing for SARS-CoV-2. Due to the absence of signal amplification, this simplicity comes at a cost in sensitivity. Here, we enhance sensitivity by developing an amplified lateral flow assay that incorporates isothermal, enzyme-free signal amplification based on the mechanism of hybridization chain reaction (HCR). The simplicity of the user experience is maintained using a disposable 3-channel lateral flow device to automatically deliver reagents to the test region in three successive stages without user interaction. To perform a test, the user loads the sample, closes the device, and reads the result by eye after 60 minutes. Detecting gamma-irradiated SARS-CoV-2 virions in a mixture of saliva and extraction buffer, the current amplified HCR lateral flow assay achieves a limit of detection of 200 copies/
                  <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                  L using available antibodies to target the SARS-CoV-2 nucleocapsid protein. By comparison, five commercial unamplified lateral flow assays that use proprietary antibodies exhibit limits of detection of 500 copies/
                  <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                  L, 1000 copies/
                  <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                  L, 2000 copies/
                  <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                  L, 2000 copies/
                  <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                  L, and 20,000 copies/
                  <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                  L. By swapping out antibody probes to target different pathogens, amplified HCR lateral flow assays offer a platform for simple, rapid, and sensitive at-home testing for infectious disease. As an alternative to viral protein detection, we further introduce an HCR lateral flow assay for viral RNA detection.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>HCR lateral flow assay</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Amplified</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Instrument-free</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>At-home</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>60 min</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Naked eye</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>SARS-CoV-2</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        200 copies/
                        <jats:italic>μ</jats:italic>
                        L
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                  <jats:fig id="ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait" fig-type="figure">
                    <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="508442v1_ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                  </jats:fig>
                </jats:sec>
title: HCR Lateral Flow Assays for Amplified Instrument-Free At-Home SARS-CoV-2 Testing
